codeReview.summary: The change adds a focused unit test and documentation, but it does not deliver the required smoke-path evidence through a real app-entry-to-active-task path. The new test only verifies panel command ordering around `startReviewerTask`, while the handoff and browser evidence remained blocked by an empty inbox.

issues.description: The added test only asserts panel command ordering around `startReviewerTask()` and checks that the fixture task object still carries `formId`, attachments, and workflow history. Because `setCurrentTask` is mocked as a no-op, it never verifies that the real reviewer workbench hydrates and renders that handed-off context, so it does not prove INT-008's active-task continuity requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker could not complete the skill's required manual reviewer smoke path, but the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true. The skill procedure and review expectations are clear; the gap is that the feature outcome did not acknowledge the unmet manual-validation step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/review-entry-worker/SKILL.md` step 9 requires validating at least one task selection plus one workflow action path, while the handoff for worker session d0929d4f-2663-4af7-874c-29c39a1067e7 says the live dataset exposed no reviewer task card to select and no real workflow mutation could be exercised.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state docs still overstate cross-surface readiness: they mention live review task data and a full smoke path, but do not distinguish generic backend reachability from reviewer-task seed readiness needed to reach an active task in live validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff reports an empty reviewer inbox during live validation, while `.factory/library/environment.md` says `GET /api/review/tasks` returned live review task data during planning and `.factory/library/user-testing.md:31` prescribes opening the same task in the reviewer workspace as part of the smoke path.

summary: Fail: the feature adds unit-level handoff coverage, but that coverage stops short of proving real active-task hydration and continuity in the reviewer workbench. INT-008 requires an unbroken smoke path from app entry to an active reviewer task; this feature's evidence remains insufficient because the new test only checks panel sequencing and fixture shape, not real active-task rendering or continuity.
